data.text: a widely cultivated shrub (Camellia sinensis of the family Theaceae, the tea family) native to China, northern India, and southeastern Asia and having glossy green leaves and fragrant white flowers

data.text: a bag usually of filter paper holding enough tea for an individual serving
a perforated metal ball that holds tea leaves and is used in brewing tea in a pot or cup

a small cylindrical candle that is usually less than 1 inch in height and that is usually set in a holder (as of metal or plastic); also: a battery-operated artificial candle of similar size and shape —sometimes hyphenated before another noun
examples: Tiny tea lights float on water in the tub in the darkened bathroom.
We like this ceramic fondue pot that uses a simple tea light candle to keep the cheese melty and delicious.
You could create a whole miniature village of houses, each one with a battery-powered tealight inside.
